name: Rasterio

performance.notes: Local file I/O; performance depends on file format and size. Cloud-optimized GeoTIFF (COG) enables efficient partial reads from S3/GCS.

agent_readiness.notes: Python library for reading and writing raster geospatial data (GeoTIFF, NetCDF, HDF5, etc.) via GDAL bindings. Clean NumPy-based API for working with raster bands, windows, masks, and reprojection. Supports Cloud-Optimized GeoTIFFs (COG) for efficient S3/GCS access. Used in satellite imagery processing, elevation models, and environmental data pipelines. Pairs well with NumPy, SciPy, and Shapely.

gotchas: CRS (coordinate reference system) handling complex — EPSG codes vs WKT vs PROJ strings
Memory management for large files requires windowed reading
GDAL version dependency issues on some platforms
Reprojection requires understanding of CRS transforms
best_when: Processing satellite imagery or aerial photography (GeoTIFF, COG)
Elevation model (DEM) processing and terrain analysis
Raster-vector overlay analysis (burning vectors into rasters)
Environmental data processing (climate, land cover, remote sensing)
avoid_when: Simple image processing without geospatial context (use PIL/Pillow)
Very large distributed raster datasets (consider Xarray + Dask)
summary: Rasterio is the standard Python library for reading and writing geospatial raster data. Clean NumPy-based API wrapping GDAL. Supports GeoTIFF, NetCDF, HDF5, and Cloud-Optimized GeoTIFFs for S3/GCS streaming. Used in satellite imagery processing, elevation analysis, and environmental data pipelines. Pairs with Shapely for raster-vector operations. Essential for geospatial data science.
